caption: Newton's third-law collision forces (closes #678): melee knockback now conserves momentum bidirectionally. Before this PR only the struck defender was shoved; now the attacker also recoils along the same strike axis, and the impulse splits between the two by effective mass (bracing raises effective mass, so a braced defender both moves less AND kicks back harder). This clip is the existing cavalry-into-braced-infantry contact scenario, framed close on the front rank: watch the horses visibly rock backward on each contact tick, not just the infantry line giving ground -- that recoil is the new behavior. Bracing still caps how much of the shove reaches a heavily-braced deep file (fixed this round to scale smoothly instead of snapping on at the capacity threshold), but never reduces the attacker's own recoil. Verified tick-by-tick via a state dump (not just this clip) -- see the state transcript comment on this PR.
